# Small Task

## Goal

What is the smallest useful thing we are trying to build or improve?

## Why

Why does this task matter for HomeOps or for learning the workflow?

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The task has one clear purpose.
- [ ] The expected result is written in beginner-friendly language.
- [ ] There is a clear way to verify the result.

## Out of scope

What should we avoid changing in this issue?

## Likely files

Which files are expected to change?

- `TBD`

## Suggested branch

```text
feature/000-short-name
```

## Edge cases

What can go wrong or be misunderstood?

- [ ] Invalid input
- [ ] Missing required data
- [ ] External service unavailable
- [ ] Permission denied
- [ ] Empty result
- [ ] Duplicate data
- [ ] Timeout or network failure

## Error handling

How should the app or documentation respond when something goes wrong?

## Beginner notes

What concepts should be explained while doing this task?

## Verification

How do we know this works?

- [ ] Manual verification steps are written.
- [ ] Automated test command is listed, if relevant.
- [ ] `git status` and `git diff` are reviewed before commit.

## Pull request notes

What should the reviewer focus on?

## Learning log

- [ ] Update `docs/learning-log.md` after the task is complete, if the task taught something meaningful.
